title: Rethinking whole-body CT image interpretation: an abnormality-centric approach
authors_text: Z Zhao, L Dai, Y Zhang, W Xie, Y Wang

title: Predicting Infarct Outcomes After Extended Time Window Thrombectomy in Large Vessel Occlusion Using Knowledge-Guided Deep Learning
authors_text: Lisong Dai, Lei Yuan, Houwang Zhang, Zheng Sun, Jingxuan Jiang, Zhaohui Li, Yuehua Li, Yunfei Zha.
abstract: This study develops a knowledge-guided deep learning model to predict final infarct outcomes after extended time window thrombectomy in large vessel occlusion. By incorporating clinical and imaging knowledge priors, the model supports patient selection for reperfusion therapy in the extended treatment window.

title: LumbarSR: A Paired Clinical CT and Photon-Counting Micro-CT Dataset for Human Lumbar Vertebrae
authors_text: Ping Wang*, Ruipeng Zhang*, Mengfei Wang, Shenyan Zong, Jinyu Zhu, Xinyu Song, Zhenzhen Cao, Xuefei Hu, Dan Wang, Yuehua Li.
abstract: LumbarSR provides the first paired clinical CT and photon-counting micro-CT dataset of human lumbar vertebrae, enabling super-resolution benchmarks that bridge the resolution gap between in-vivo imaging and high-resolution ex-vivo micro-CT. The dataset and challenge foster reproducible evaluation of medical image super-resolution methods.

title: Evaluation of Large Language Models for Diagnostic Impression Generation from Brain MRI Report Findings: A Multicenter Benchmark and Reader Study
authors_text: Ming-Liang Wang*, Rui-Peng Zhang*, Wen-Juan Wu*, Yu Lu*, Xiao-Er Wei, Zheng Sun, Bao-Hui Guan, Jun-Jie Zhang, Xue Wu, Lei Zhang#, Tian-Le Wang#, Yue-Hua Li#. * Co-first authors. # Corresponding authors.
abstract: Benchmarked 10 large language models on generating diagnostic impressions from brain MRI report findings across a multicenter dataset, establishing a reproducible evaluation framework and reader study for LLM-based radiology reporting.

title: Enhancing bone metastasis CT report analysis: a comparison of local and proprietary large language models for privacy and resource efficiency
authors_text: Zhuo Li*, Mengfei Wang*, Ao Liu, Yao Zeng, Bicong Yan, Zhongzheng Cao, Jinyu Zhu, Yulu Yang, Yuehua Li. * Co-first authors.

title: A Multi-Agent Large Language Model Framework for Emergency Treatment Recommendation in Acute Ischemic Stroke: Development and Validation Study
authors_text: Bicong Yan, Ruipeng Zhang, Yanfeng Fan, Ying Li, Li Chen, Xinyu Song, Yixiao Tang, Zhongzheng Cao, Li Shen, Mengfei Wang, Zhuo Li, Yijia Xiong, Yuehua Li.
abstract: Acute ischemic stroke requires rapid, guideline-driven treatment decisions within narrow time windows. This study presents a multi-agent large language model framework (HR-LLM-Stroke) that orchestrates specialized agents for imaging interpretation, clinical feature extraction, and treatment recommendation, supporting real-world clinical decision-making in acute ischemic stroke. Developed and validated on multicenter data, the framework demonstrates the feasibility of agentic LLM systems for time-critical emergency stroke workflows.

title: UniBrain: Universal brain MRI diagnosis with hierarchical knowledge-enhanced pre-training
authors_text: J Lei, L Dai, H Jiang, C Wu, X Zhang, et al.
abstract: Hierarchical knowledge-enhanced pre-training framework for universal brain MRI diagnosis, validated on in-domain and out-of-domain datasets.

title: Expanding point cloud statistical shape model applications: Generalized vascular modeling for population-level hemodynamic simulations
authors_text: Yao Zeng, Zheng Sun, Mengfei Wang, Zhuo Li, Ao Liu, Meixiu Pan, Haifeng Zhao, Yuehua Li.

title: Deep learning-based automatic ASPECTS calculation can improve diagnosis efficiency in patients with acute ischemic stroke: a multicenter study
authors_text: J Wei, K Shang, X Wei, … L Dai, Z Sun, … Y Li
abstract: Deep-learning ASPECTS scoring on non-contrast CT improves diagnostic efficiency for acute ischemic stroke across multiple centers.

title: Biomimetic microenvironment nanomodulators for reactive oxygen species regulation
authors_text: … Y Li, et al.
abstract: ROS-responsive T1-T2 dual-modal MRI nanomodulators for visualized treatment of atherosclerosis.

title: UniChest: Conquer-and-Divide Pre-training for Multi-Source Chest X-Ray Classification
authors_text: Tianjie Dai, Ruipeng Zhang, Feng Hong, Jiangchao Yao, Ya Zhang, Yanfeng Wang.

title: Synchronous Superficial Middle Cerebral Vein Outflow Correlates Favorable Tissue Fate After Mechanical Thrombectomy for Acute Ischemic Stroke
authors_text: Lisong Dai, Zheng Sun, Jingxuan Jiang, Jianyong Wei, Xinyu Song, Shen Chen, Yuehua Li.
abstract: This study investigates the relationship between superficial middle cerebral vein (SMCV) outflow patterns and tissue outcomes following mechanical thrombectomy in acute ischemic stroke. Synchronous SMCV outflow was found to correlate with favorable tissue fate, supporting venous outflow assessment as an imaging marker for prognosis after reperfusion therapy.

title: Precision Structuring of Free-Text Surgical Record for Enhanced Stroke Management: A Comparative Evaluation of Large Language Models
authors_text: Mengfei Wang, Jianyong Wei, Yao Zeng, Lisong Dai, Bicong Yan, Yueqi Zhu, Xiaoer Wei, Yidong Jin, Yuehua Li.

abstract: A foundation-model approach addressing the long-tailed distribution of radiology diagnoses, improving recognition of rare conditions on medical images.

abstract: Knowledge-augmented deep learning to segment and detect cerebral aneurysms on CT angiography, validated in a multicenter study with stable external performance.

title: AutoRG-Brain: Grounded report generation for brain MRI
authors_text: J Lei, X Zhang, C Wu, L Dai, … Y Li
abstract: The first brain MRI report generation system with pixel-level grounded visual clues, using a two-stage segmentation-then-generation framework.

title: A Dual-Energy Computed Tomography-Based Radiomics Nomogram for Predicting Time Since Stroke Onset: A Multicenter Study
authors_text: Jingxuan Jiang, Kai Sheng, Minda Li, Huilin Zhao, Baohui Guan, Lisong Dai, Yuehua Li.
abstract: Accurate estimation of time since stroke onset is critical for treatment eligibility decisions. This multicenter study develops a dual-energy CT-based radiomics nomogram to predict onset time, combining quantitative iodine maps and radiomic features. The model provides a clinically actionable tool for refining treatment windows in acute ischemic stroke.

title: The Value of Dual-Energy Computed Tomography Angiography-Derived Parameters in the Evaluation of Clot Composition
authors_text: Jingxuan Jiang, Hongmei Gu, Minda Li, Ye Hua, Sijia Wang, Lisong Dai, Yuehua Li.
abstract: This study evaluates dual-energy CT angiography-derived parameters for assessing clot composition in acute ischemic stroke, demonstrating the added value of dual-energy acquisition over conventional imaging for thrombus characterization and treatment planning.

title: SimDE: A Simple Domain Expansion Approach for Single-Source Domain Generalization
authors_text: Qinwei Xu, Ruipeng Zhang, Yi-Yan Wu, Ya Zhang, Ning Liu, Yanfeng Wang.

title: Redox-Responsive Peptide-Based Complex Coacervates as Delivery Vehicles with Controlled Release of Proteinous Drugs
authors_text: Jiahua Wang, Manzar Abbas, Yu Huang, Junyou Wang, Yuehua Li.
abstract: Proteinous drugs are highly promising therapeutics but suffer from limited circulation times and severe off-target side effects. Inspired by active membraneless organelles capable of dynamic recruitment and release of specific proteins, this study designs coacervates as therapeutic protocells from small metabolites, providing redox-responsive controlled release of proteinous drugs.

title: Qualitative and Quantitative Stress Perfusion Cardiac Magnetic Resonance in Clinical Practice: A Comprehensive Review
authors_text: Wenli Zhou, Jason Sin, Andrew T. Yan, Haonan Wang, Jing Lu, Yuehua Li, Paul Kim, Amit R. Patel, Ming-Yen Ng.
abstract: This comprehensive review covers qualitative and quantitative stress perfusion cardiac magnetic resonance methods in clinical practice, including acquisition protocols, analysis techniques, and clinical applications for ischemic heart disease assessment.

title: Prevalence of unruptured cerebral aneurysms in Chinese adults aged 35 to 75 years: a cross-sectional study
authors_text: … Y Li, et al.
abstract: Cross-sectional study reporting a 7.0% prevalence of unruptured cerebral aneurysms in Chinese adults aged 35–75, with most lesions <5 mm.

authors_text: S Wang, D Wu, L Ye, Z Chen, Y Zhan, Y Li
abstract: Deep-learning algorithm for automatic rib fracture detection on chest CT with high sensitivity, assessed for clinical deployment.

title: Transferrin-Enabled Blood–Brain Barrier Crossing Manganese-Based Nanozyme for Rebalancing the Reactive Oxygen Species Level in Ischemic Stroke
authors_text: Qianqian Zhao, Wenxian Du, Lingling Zhou, Jianrong Wu, Xiaoxing Zhang, Xiaoer Wei, Sijia Wang, Yu Huang, Yuehua Li.
abstract: This study presents a transferrin-enabled, manganese-based nanozyme that crosses the blood–brain barrier to rebalance reactive oxygen species levels in ischemic stroke, offering a theranostic nanomedicine approach combining antioxidant therapy with manganese-based imaging contrast.

authors_text: … Y Li, et al.
abstract: Prussian blue nanozyme promotes skin flap survival by scavenging ROS and maintaining a normal microenvironment against ischemia-reperfusion injury.

authors_text: Dongya Wang, Jingcan Qin, Chuan Zhang, Yuehua Li.
abstract: This work reports a facile synthesis of black phosphorus nanosheet@NaReF4 nanocomposites for potential bioimaging applications, combining the photothermal and photodynamic properties of black phosphorus with rare-earth-doped upconversion nanoparticles.

title: Engineering vanadium carbide MXene as multienzyme mimetics for efficient in vivo ischemic stroke treatment

abstract: Engineered vanadium carbide MXene as multienzyme mimetics for efficient in vivo ischemic stroke treatment by alleviating oxidative stress.

abstract: This study examines the associations of plasma phosphorylated tau181 and neurofilament light chain with brain amyloid burden and cognition in patients with objectively defined subtle cognitive decline, exploring blood biomarkers for early Alzheimer's disease detection.

title: Inorganic Nanomaterial for Biomedical Imaging of Brain Diseases
authors_text: Wenxian Du, Lingling Zhou, Qiang Zhang, Xin Liu, Xiaoer Wei, Yuehua Li.
abstract: This review surveys inorganic nanomaterials used for biomedical imaging of brain diseases, covering design principles, imaging modalities, and translational considerations for nanomaterial-based neuroimaging probes.

title: Experimental Imaging Study of Encephalomalacia Fluid-Attenuated Inversion Recovery (FLAIR) Hyperintense Lesions in Posttraumatic Epilepsy
authors_text: Dan Wang, Kai Shang, Zheng Sun, Yue-Hua Li.
abstract: This study applies advanced MRI techniques, including neurite orientation dispersion and density imaging (NODDI), to characterize encephalomalacia FLAIR hyperintense lesions in posttraumatic epilepsy. The three-compartment tissue model enables examination of intra- and extracellular white matter properties, distinguishing aspects of axonal pathology underlying posttraumatic epileptogenic lesions.

title: Transient Ischemic Attack and Carotid Web
authors_text: H. Hu, X. Zhang, J. Zhao, Y. Li, Y. Zhao.
abstract: This study describes the association between carotid web—a thin, fibrous intimal shelf in the internal carotid artery—and transient ischemic attack, highlighting the vascular imaging features of this under-recognized cause of ischemic stroke in young patients.
